The Python code uses integer division (//) which matches Coq's Z division (/) for integers. Both compute res == (n + 1) * n / 2, returning the same boolean judgment for all concrete integer inputs.

Both check res equals ((n+1)*n)/2 with integer division; Coq Z.div by 2 matches Python // for all integers, so results agree for all inputs.
Both check res equals ((n+1)*n)/2 over integers. Since (n+1)*n is always even and divisor is 2 (>0), Coq’s Z.div and Python’s // coincide, so results match for all inputs.
